name: Monasterio de San Pedro de Rocas – circular by the Camino Real
name_original: Monasterio de San Pedro de Rocas – circular por el Camino Real
region_intro: Jogging around Esgos offers an excellent way to explore the diverse natural landscapes of this region in Spain's Ribeira Sacra. The area is characterized by a tranquil environment of dense forests and mountainous terrain, providing scenic routes for various fitness levels. Runners can experience varied terrain, from ancient royal roads with preserved stonework to paths winding alongside rivers. The region's dramatic river gorges and vineyards also contribute to its unique outdoor appeal. Best jogging routes around Esgos The most popular jogging route is Monasterio de San Pedro de Rocas – circular by the Camino Real, a 4.8 miles (7.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 2 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows an ancient royal road through lush oak and chestnut forests, offering views of rock formations and leading to the historic Monastery of San Pedro de Rocas. Another top favourite among local runners is Ribeira Sacra Natural Trail, a difficult 11.2 miles (18.0 km) path. This route traverses the broader Ribeira Sacra region, known for its dramatic river gorges and vineyard landscapes. Local runners also love the Cachamuíña Reservoir – Cachamuíña Reservoir loop from O Pereiro de Aguiar, a 3.7 miles (5.9 km) trail leading through a reservoir area, often completed in about 37 minutes. This moderate route features minimal elevation changes, providing a comfortable experience. Jogging around Esgos is defined by mountainous terrain, dense forests, and tranquil river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from relaxed jogs to more challenging long-distance running. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 300 runners have used komoot to explore Esgos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Esgos?
A: Komoot offers &lt;strong&gt;over 30 running routes&lt;/strong&gt; around Esgos, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These include options through dense forests, mountainous terrain, and tranquil river valleys.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes in Esgos suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Esgos offers several easy running trails. The Region Research highlights the &lt;em&gt;Ruta dos Muíños do Río Grañal&lt;/em&gt; as an easy, linear option of about 7 kilometers, which can be extended to 14 kilometers out-and-back. This trail winds alongside the Grañal River, featuring minimal elevation changes and a soft dirt surface, making it ideal for a relaxed jog or family outing. Another moderate option with minimal elevation changes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24555065" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cachamuíña Reservoir – Cachamuíña Reservoir loop from O Pereiro de Aguiar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails around Esgos?
A: The running trails in Esgos offer a diverse range of terrain. You can expect to jog through lush oak and chestnut forests, along ancient royal roads with preserved stonework, and beside tranquil rivers. The region is characterized by its mountainous terrain and dense forests, providing varied surfaces from dirt paths to stony sections, especially on routes like the &lt;em&gt;Camiño Real de San Pedro de Rocas&lt;/em&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging or long-distance running trails in the Esgos area?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a more challenging experience, Esgos and the surrounding Ribeira Sacra region offer difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990597251/ribeira-sacra-natural-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribeira Sacra Natural Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 18 km path that traverses the broader region, known for its dramatic river gorges and vineyard landscapes. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20148314" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fonte de San Bito loop from Esgos&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 25 km.

Q: What historical landmarks or natural features can I see along the jogging routes?
A: Many running routes in Esgos pass by significant historical and natural attractions. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990556161/monasterio-de-san-pedro-de-rocas-circular-by-the-camino-real" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de San Pedro de Rocas – circular by the Camino Real&lt;/a&gt; leads directly to the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3136768" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of San Pedro de Rocas&lt;/a&gt;, partially excavated into rock. Other notable highlights in the area include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4213863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedra Longa Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3726234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;O Boqueiriño Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering impressive views of the surrounding landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available in Esgos?
A: Yes, circular routes are a popular option for runners in Esgos. A prominent example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990556161/monasterio-de-san-pedro-de-rocas-circular-by-the-camino-real" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de San Pedro de Rocas – circular by the Camino Real&lt;/a&gt;, a 7.7 km trail that offers a complete loop through forests and past historical sites. Another circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24555065" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cachamuíña Reservoir – Cachamuíña Reservoir loop from O Pereiro de Aguiar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Esgos?
A: Esgos offers a pleasant environment for jogging for much of the year. The lush oak and chestnut forests provide ample shade, making it enjoyable even during warmer months. However, some parts of the pathways can be stony and potentially slippery when wet, so it's advisable to check weather conditions and wear appropriate footwear, especially during rainy seasons.

Q: Are there any running trails with scenic viewpoints in Esgos?
A: Esgos is situated in the picturesque Ribeira Sacra region, known for its stunning landscapes. Many trails offer impressive views of rock formations, river gorges, and vineyards. The &lt;em&gt;Camiño Real de San Pedro de Rocas&lt;/em&gt; provides views of surrounding peaks, and the area features dedicated viewpoints such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4213863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedra Longa Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3726234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;O Boqueiriño Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which can be incorporated into longer runs or visited separately.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Esgos?
A: Runners frequently praise the tranquil environment, diverse natural landscapes, and the blend of historical and natural beauty found in Esgos. The komoot community highlights the well-maintained paths, the shade provided by dense forests, and the unique experience of running along ancient royal roads or beside serene rivers. The variety of routes, from easy riverside jogs to challenging mountain trails, is also a key draw.

Q: Are there any specific considerations for footwear on Esgos' running trails?
A: Given the varied terrain, appropriate footwear is recommended. While some paths consist of soft dirt, others, like sections of the &lt;em&gt;Camiño Real de San Pedro de Rocas&lt;/em&gt;, can be stony and potentially slippery, especially when wet. Trail running shoes with good grip are advisable to ensure comfort and safety on all surfaces.

Q: Can I find running routes that explore the broader Ribeira Sacra region from Esgos?
A: Yes, Esgos serves as an excellent starting point for exploring the wider Ribeira Sacra region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990597251/ribeira-sacra-natural-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribeira Sacra Natural Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for example, is a challenging 18 km route that takes you through the dramatic river gorges and vineyard landscapes characteristic of this stunning area.
tips: Excavated in the natural rock of the mountains near the town of Esgos in Ourense is the abandoned monastery of San Pedro de Rocas. This ancient cult building is considered one of the oldest in Galicia and the only one that still retains part of its original structure.

waypoints.name: Monastery of San Pedro de Rocas
